zh.lifeSummary: 當William Foule於大約1536年出生在英國英格蘭肯特貝嫩登時，他的父親Nicholas Fowle26歲，母親Mrs Nicholas Foule22歲。他於1570年6月19日與Elizabeth Warde在英國肯特洛爾文登結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1574年3月3日在英國肯特洛爾文登享年39歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特洛爾文登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Foule于大约1536年出生在英国英格兰肯特貝嫩登时，他的父亲Nicholas Fowle26岁，母亲Mrs Nicholas Foule22岁。他于1570年6月19日与Elizabeth Warde在英国肯特洛爾文登结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1574年3月3日在英国肯特洛爾文登享年39岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特洛爾文登。

nameMeanings.name: Foule
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Gloucestershire): of Norman origin, a variant of   Soul  .

French (Soulé): topographic name for someone who lived in a house exposed to sun, a variant of Soulier 2; or a habitational name from Soulé, the name of several places in the southwestern part of France.
History: George Soule (1600–80), one of the passengers on the Mayflower in 1620, was one of the founders of Duxbury, MA, where he became comparatively wealthy. He left eight children.

variants.name: Sowle
meanings: English: variant of   Soule  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Soul
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Soules near Saint-Lô (Manche, France).
French (mainly Manche): variant of Saoul, a nickname from saoul ‘full, satiated; drunk’, figuratively also ‘surfeited with something’.

History: The Scottish family of this name traces the name back to Ranulf de Soulis (died before 1170), a Norman knight who came to Scotland with King David I and served as his cupbearer. Sir John Soulis (died before 1310) was appointed ‘guardian of Scotland’ by John Balliol in 1301.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When William Foule was born about 1536, in Benenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom, his father, Nicholas Fowle, was 26 and his mother, Mrs Nicholas Foule, was 22. He married Elizabeth Warde on 19 June 1570, in Rolvenden, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 3 March 1574, in Rolvenden, Kent, England, at the age of 39, and was buried in Rolvenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Foule est né environ 1536, à Benenden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Nicholas Fowle, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Mrs Nicholas Foule, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Warde le 19 juin 1570, à Rolvenden, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 3 mars 1574, à Rolvenden, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterré à Rolvenden, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: William Foule(이)가 대략 1536년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 베니든에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicholas Fowle(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Nicholas Foule(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1570년 6월 19일 영국 켄트 롤븐든에서 Elizabeth Warde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1574년 3월 3일 영국 켄트 롤븐든에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 롤븐든에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Foule nasceu aproximadamente 1536, em Benenden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Nicholas Fowle, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Nicholas Foule, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Warde em 19 de junho de 1570, em Rolvenden, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 3 de março de 1574, em Rolvenden, Kent, Inglaterra, com 39 anos, e foi sepultado em Rolvenden, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Foule родился около 1536 г. в местности биненден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Nicholas Fowle было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Nicholas Foule, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Warde 19 июня 1570 г. в местности ролвенден, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер 3 марта 1574 г. в местности ролвенден, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 39 лет и был похоронен в местности ролвенден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
